recipe.title: Sushi Bowl

ingredients.name: cooked brown or white rice
ingredients.note: Season while still warm for shortcut sushi rice.

ingredients.name: baked tofu
ingredients.note: Linked source uses 14 ounces extra-firm tofu, olive oil, tamari, sriracha, optional cornstarch, baked at 425 F for 20 to 25 minutes until browned around the edges.

ingredients.name: avocados
ingredients.note: Sliced; about half an avocado per serving bowl.

ingredients.name: pickled ginger
ingredients.note: Small visible curls for peppery freshness.

ingredients.name: pickled radishes
ingredients.note: Included in this plan for pink color contrast unless the operator removes the optional component.

ingredients.name: thinly sliced nori or seaweed snacks
ingredients.note: Thin strips or crumbled seaweed snack garnish; crackly texture.

ingredients.name: microgreens
ingredients.note: Small fresh tuft; keep quantity controlled.

ingredients.name: spicy mayo
ingredients.note: Linked source uses mayonnaise, sriracha, lime juice, and toasted sesame oil; planned as orange glossy drizzle.

steps.text: Make or stage the spicy mayo by stirring mayonnaise, sriracha, lime juice, and toasted sesame oil until glossy.
steps.timing: 5 minutes if made from linked recipe
steps.visualCue: orange cream folds into a shiny, pipeable sauce
steps.textureCue: thick glossy ribbon with tiny red sriracha streaks before fully mixed
steps.runwayRisk: Sauce can look like ketchup unless mayo opacity and sesame oil sheen are described.

steps.text: Press extra-firm tofu if needed, then pat dry.
steps.timing: 20 to 30 minutes pressing if tofu is not pre-pressed
steps.visualCue: water beads on towel, dense pale tofu block
steps.runwayRisk: Pressing action can become vague; compress it to a quick setup beat.
Scale drift can turn tofu into giant blocks or tiny dice.

steps.text: Toss tofu with olive oil, tamari, sriracha, and optional cornstarch.
steps.textureCue: thin glossy coating, optional powder disappearing
steps.runwayRisk: Cornstarch can look like raw flour if not shown becoming absorbed.

steps.text: Bake tofu at 425 F for 20 to 25 minutes until browned around the edges.
steps.visualCue: golden edges and light crisp corners on cubes
steps.textureCue: firm chewy centers, crisp browned corners, light oil sheen
steps.runwayRisk: Oven transformation should be a hard cut from raw coated cubes to browned cubes.

steps.text: Cook rice separately, then season while still warm with rice vinegar, cane sugar, and salt.
steps.visualCue: steam, vinegar pour, sugar crystals dissolving
steps.runwayRisk: Rice can look dry unless steam and vinegar sheen are emphasized.

steps.visualCue: green fan slices with pale center and darker rim
steps.textureCue: creamy smooth avocado, clean knife cuts
steps.runwayRisk: Avocado can brown or become mush if the slices are not locked.

steps.visualCue: bright orange cubes falling from knife
steps.runwayRisk: Mango may be confused with tofu unless color and juiciness are clear.

steps.text: Thinly slice Persian cucumbers and scallions.
steps.visualCue: pale green cucumber coins and fine green onion rings
steps.textureCue: crisp watery cucumber snap, delicate scallion rings
steps.runwayRisk: Tiny garnish drift if counts and placement are vague.

steps.text: Use pickled radishes if desired; linked quick pickle uses thin slices, hot brine, and cooling until bright pink.
steps.timing: 15 minutes prep plus at least 1 hour chill for slices
steps.visualCue: white-red radish slices turn vivid pink in clear jar
steps.textureCue: thin crisp wet slices in transparent brine
steps.runwayRisk: Optional component can be omitted by the model unless treated as a deliberate visual accent.

steps.text: Divide seasoned rice among four bowls.
steps.visualCue: warm rice base mounded into serving bowl
steps.textureCue: sticky rice base filling the bottom third of a 16 to 18 cm bowl
steps.runwayRisk: Bowl proportions can drift without explicit serving-bowl scale.

steps.text: Top each bowl with baked tofu, avocado, mango, cucumber, scallions, pickled ginger, and pickled radishes if using.
steps.textureCue: crisp, creamy, juicy, chewy contrasts in one bowl
steps.runwayRisk: Too many tiny toppings can become confetti unless each amount is controlled.

steps.text: Garnish with nori, sesame seeds, microgreens, and spicy mayo.
steps.visualCue: black nori strips, sesame rain, orange mayo zigzag
steps.textureCue: crackly seaweed, glossy sauce, small fresh greens
steps.runwayRisk: Nori can appear as full sheets; lock thin strips and no wrappers.

steps.text: Serve immediately while rice and tofu are warm and fresh toppings remain crisp.
steps.visualCue: hero bowl on Licorn kitchen island, character satisfied
steps.textureCue: warm-cool contrast, glossy sauce, crisp garnish
steps.runwayRisk: Final dish should remain a bowl, not sushi rolls.

assumptions: The plan includes optional pickled radishes because their bright pink color strengthens the visual contrast.
The spicy mayo is treated as regular mayonnaise unless the operator later requests the vegan version.
The finished serving vessel is a single 16 to 18 cm bowl per portion, not sushi rolls or a platter.
The tofu is locked as 1-inch or 2.5 cm cubes, about 8 to 10 visible cubes per bowl, not large steaks or tiny crumbles.
Fresh toppings are staged as controlled blocks: about half an avocado, half a mango, and half a Persian cucumber per bowl.
criticalTransformations: Warm rice absorbs rice vinegar, sugar, and salt and becomes glossy sticky seasoned rice.
Extra-firm tofu changes from matte pale 1-inch cubes to tamari-sriracha coated cubes with browned crisp edges.
Spicy mayo changes from separate mayonnaise, sriracha, lime juice, and sesame oil into a thick orange glossy drizzle.
Pickled radish slices shift from white-red raw rounds to bright pink crisp slices in clear brine.
Fresh avocado, mango, cucumber, scallions, ginger, nori, sesame, and microgreens assemble into a controlled radial bowl rather than loose salad confetti.
visualTextureOpportunities: Orange spicy mayo ribbon lifting from a spoon or squeeze bottle before the story resets to components.
Vinegar splashing over steaming rice and turning grains glossy.
Tamari-sriracha glaze clinging to tofu cubes before a hard cut to browned crisp edges.
Avocado fan slices sliding beside juicy mango cubes and crisp cucumber coins.
Pink radish slices floating against clear brine in a glass jar.
Black nori strips crumpling and crackling over the bowl.
Sesame seed rain catching on glossy mayo and sticky rice.
Final spoon lift showing sticky rice, browned tofu, avocado, mango, cucumber, nori, and sauce in one bite.
possibleHooks: Glossy spicy mayo ribbon falls in slow zigzag over black nori strips, then a hard cut reveals the warm rice base.
Crisp tofu cubes tumble onto sticky seasoned rice with an audible tap.
A bright pink pickled radish slice slides through clear brine before the colorful bowl assembles.
A spoon lifts a perfect bite with rice strings, tofu edge, avocado, mango, and orange sauce.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the optional pickled radishes stay in the production version?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: They materially affect color blocking, reference planning, and the pink brine texture beat.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the spicy mayo be presented as vegan mayo or regular mayo?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: The source allows a vegan version; the visual sauce is similar, but labeling and ingredient assumptions differ.
